claim_boundary: This validates AANA platform integration parity, audit-safe decision shape, and blocked-tool non-execution across local surfaces. It is not a raw agent-performance benchmark.

dataset_sources.split_boundary: Held-out platform validation only; do not tune on these cases.
Held-out platform validation only; do not tune on these cases.
Held-out platform validation only; do not tune on these cases.
Held-out platform validation only; do not tune on these cases.
Held-out platform validation only; do not tune on these cases.

limitations: Held-out cases are small and focused on platform parity, not broad task success.
HF-derived cases are redacted schema-style transformations, not full benchmark replay.
Latency is local runtime latency and should not be treated as production deployment latency.
No tuning was performed on these held-out integration cases.

split_policy.notes: These cases validate platform-surface parity and enforcement behavior. They must not be used for adapter threshold tuning.
